# AI Agent Guidelines

The following sections describe this project's conventions and patterns.
Generated automatically by [skillgen](https://github.com/skillgen/skillgen).

## Naming Conventions

# Naming Conventions

This project (Python) follows these naming conventions.

## Function Naming
- Functions use snake_case
  - Examples: `analyze_project (analyzer.py)`, `func (analyzer.py)`

## Class/Type Naming
- Classes/types use snake_case
  - Examples: `naming (analyzer.py)`
- Classes/types use PascalCase
  - Examples: `TestNameClassification (test_analyzer.py)`, `TestNamingExtraction (test_analyzer.py)`, `UserService (test_analyzer.py)`

## File Naming
- Python files: `snake_case.py`


## Error Handling

# Error Handling

Error handling conventions for Python.

## Exception Types
- Uses try/except with types: patterns, with, OSError
  - `except OSError`
  - `except patterns`
- Uses try/except with types: typer, KeyboardInterrupt, Exception, errors
  - `except errors`
  - `except typer`
- Uses try/except with types: Exception
  - `except Exception`
  - `except Exception`

## Raise Style
- Raises: typer
  - `raise typer`
  - `raise typer`
- Raises: ValueError, with, OSError
  - `raise OSError`
  - `raise ValueError`
- Raises: def
  - `raise def`

## Custom Exceptions
- Defines custom exceptions: ValidationError, NotFoundError, ValidationError
  - `class ValidationError`
  - `class NotFoundError`
- Defines custom exceptions: ValidationError, NotFoundError
  - `class ValidationError`
  - `class NotFoundError`

## Guidelines
- Always catch specific exception types, not bare `except:`
- Use custom exception classes for domain errors
- Re-raise with `raise ... from err` to preserve tracebacks


## Testing

# Testing

Testing conventions for Python.

## Test Framework
- Uses pytest as the test framework

## Test File Naming
- Example: `test_analyzer.py`
- Example: `test_generator.py`
- Example: `test_detector.py`

## Assertion Style
- Uses self.assert* methods (unittest style)
- Uses plain assert statements (pytest style)

## Fixtures
- Uses pytest fixtures (2 in this file)
- Uses pytest fixtures (1 in this file)
- Define shared fixtures in `conftest.py` at each directory level

## Parameterized Tests
- Uses pytest.mark.parametrize for test parameterization

## General Guidelines
- Keep tests focused: one behavior per test function
- Use descriptive test names that explain the expected behavior
- Arrange-Act-Assert pattern for test structure


## Imports And Dependencies

# Imports And Dependencies

Import and dependency conventions for Python.

## Import Style
- Uses absolute imports exclusively
  - `from collections import ...`
  - `from pathlib import ...`
- Uses relative imports (27% of imports)
  - `from . import ...`
  - `from .models import ...`

## Export Control
- Uses __all__ to control public exports

## Import Order
- Group imports in this order:
  1. Standard library imports
  2. Third-party imports
  3. Local/project imports
- Separate groups with a blank line


## Documentation

# Documentation

Documentation conventions for Python.

## Docstring Style
- Uses simple docstrings (15 found)
- Uses simple docstrings (17 found)
- Uses simple docstrings (2 found)

## Module-Level Documentation
- Every module should have a top-level docstring explaining its purpose

## General Guidelines
- Document the 'why', not the 'what'
- Keep documentation close to the code it describes
- Update documentation when changing code behavior


## Architecture

# Architecture

Project architecture for Python.

## Project Structure
- Top-level directories: .claude, .cursor, .github, docs, examples, skillgen, tests

```
  .claude/
  .cursor/
  .github/
  docs/
  examples/
  skillgen/
  tests/
```

## Test Organization
- Tests organized in: tests/

## Where to Put New Code
- New modules go in the appropriate package under the source directory
- New tests go in the corresponding `tests/` subdirectory


## Code Style

# Code Style

Code style conventions for Python.

## Line Length
- Lines generally within 120 characters
- All lines within 88 characters (Black default)

## Quote Style
- Prefers double quotes

## Type Annotations
- Uses type hints (13 param annotations, 13 return annotations)
- Uses type hints (2 param annotations, 14 return annotations)
- All new functions must include type annotations for parameters and return values

## Trailing Commas
- Uses trailing commas in collections and function args

## Formatters and Linters


## Logging And Observability

# Logging And Observability

Logging and observability conventions for Python.

## Logging Library
- Uses Python standard logging module
- Uses structlog for structured logging

## Logger Initialization
- Logger initialized as: logger = logging.getLogger(...)

## Log Levels in Use
- Uses log level: debug (1 calls)
- Uses log level: error (4 calls)
- Uses log level: info (1 calls)

## Guidelines
- Use structured logging where possible (key-value pairs)
- Include request/correlation IDs in log entries
- Use appropriate log levels: DEBUG for development, INFO for operations, ERROR for failures
- Never log sensitive data (passwords, tokens, PII)
